doctrinal.description: The legal reasoning operation being performed

moves.NARROW_STATUTE: Confine a statute's scope to exclude the case at hand
moves.BROADEN_STATUTE: Expand a statute's reach to include the case at hand
moves.NARROW_HOLDING: Contract what a precedent stands for
moves.BROADEN_HOLDING: Expand what a precedent stands for
moves.DISTINGUISH: Show why a cited case doesn't apply to these facts
moves.ANALOGIZE: Show why a favorable case controls these facts
moves.APPLY_STANDARD: Walk through elements of an established legal test
moves.APPLY_TO_FACTS: Map a legal rule onto the specific facts of this case
moves.BURDEN_SHIFT: Argue the other side bears the obligation to prove something
moves.INVOKE_AUTHORITY: Cite a court, judge count, or institutional weight for persuasive force
moves.DISTINGUISH_PURPOSE: Draw a line between two statutory or doctrinal purposes
moves.CONSTITUTIONAL_AVOIDANCE: Argue for a reading of a statute that avoids a constitutional question
moves.JURISDICTIONAL_REFRAME: Swap the entire governing legal framework rather than adjusting one rule's scope
moves.SLIPPERY_SLOPE: Argue the opponent's proposed rule would produce unacceptable downstream consequences
moves.ALTERNATIVE_HOLDING: Even-if argument that the outcome should be the same regardless of which legal standard applies
moves.STARE_DECISIS_SHIELD: Use the standard for overruling precedent as an affirmative barrier to the opponent's position
moves.INTRA_STATUTORY_CONTRAST: Read neighboring provisions within the same statute against each other to show deliberate word choices
moves.SUPERFLUITY_ARGUMENT: Multi-provision surplusage canon deployed as architectural evidence of statutory meaning
moves.STRAW_MAN_EXPOSURE: Deny the factual predicate of the opponent's legal challenge — the standard being attacked was never adopted

narrative.description: The story or structural strategy being deployed

moves.AFFIRMATIVE_FRAME: State your position as a declarative fact rather than a rebuttal of the opponent
moves.CONCEDE_AND_REDIRECT: Acknowledge a weak point, then pivot to why it doesn't matter
moves.PREEMPTIVE_REBUTTAL: Address the opponent's strongest anticipated point before they make it
moves.ESCALATE_STAKES: Frame the case as having implications beyond the immediate parties
moves.FRAME_AS_ERROR: Label the lower court's decision as wrong from the opening sentence
moves.INSTITUTIONAL_REBUKE: Criticize an institutional actor for inconsistency or overreach
moves.AGREEMENT_TRAP: Use the opponent's own procedural concession to foreclose a substantive position
moves.DILEMMA_CONSTRUCTION: Frame the opponent's position as a lose-lose regardless of which path they take
moves.STRATEGIC_SHIFT_EXPOSURE: Document the opponent's change in theory between stages of litigation
moves.WAIVER_TRAP: Weaponize the opponent's prior litigation strategy as a vehicle defect
moves.SELF_REFUTING_DEFENSE: Turn the opponent's own defense into evidence against their position

fact_framing.description: How facts are selected, ordered, and characterized

moves.FOREGROUND: Lead with or emphasize a favorable fact
moves.BACKGROUND: Bury an unfavorable fact in a dependent clause, footnote, or parenthetical
moves.RECHARACTERIZE: Describe the same event using different framing language
moves.SEQUENCE: Control the timeline order of facts to imply causation or urgency
moves.STRATEGIC_SILENCE: Deliberately omit a fact where the absence won't draw attention
moves.FOREGROUND_ABSENCE: Emphasize what hasn't happened to show irrelevance or inaction
moves.DIMINISHING_MODIFIER: Insert adjectives that systematically minimize the significance of opposing facts
moves.OPPONENT_CONCESSION_CAPTURE: Foreground a concession from the opposing brief and deploy it offensively
moves.SILENCE_AS_CONCESSION: Convert the opponent's failure to contest a point into a tacit admission
moves.PROCESS_LEGITIMATION: Detailed narration of an institutional process as self-legitimating evidence
moves.ACCUMULATION_OVERWHELM: Stack multiple supporting facts in rapid succession to create cumulative weight

stylistic.description: How the move is delivered for rhetorical effect

moves.COMPRESSION: Say in one sentence what the opponent took a paragraph to say — signals confidence
moves.CADENCE_SHIFT: Follow a complex, heavily-cited passage with a short declarative sentence for emphasis
moves.CONCRETE_ANCHOR: Ground an abstract legal principle in a specific, vivid factual detail
moves.UNDERSTATED_DEVASTATION: Deliver the most damaging point in calm, clinical language
moves.STRATEGIC_QUOTATION: Let the opponent's own words or a court's language make the point for you
moves.RHETORICAL_QUESTION: Pose a question where the answer is obvious, forcing the reader to supply your conclusion
moves.PARALLEL_STRUCTURE: Line up multiple points in identical syntactic form for cumulative weight
moves.REGISTER_CONTRAST: Shift from formal legal register to colloquial or dismissive language to signal an argument is unworthy of serious engagement
moves.AUTHORITY_SIGNALING: Cite a case for the identity of its author rather than just the holding
moves.AUTHORITY_INVERSION: Capture an opponent's cited authority and deploy it for the opposite proposition

additional_attributes.risk_profile: spectrum from chess (deterministic, law is clear) to poker (probabilistic, managing audience uncertainty)
